asciichat_error_t options_init (int argc, char **
  argv)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/options/options.h>


Initialize options by parsing command-line arguments with unified
    mode detection.


Parameters


argc Argument count from main()


argv Argument vector from main()


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, ERROR_USAGE on parse
  errors


Main Entry Point for the options system. This function:





  
1.

  
Mode Detection: Analyzes argv to detect requested mode:

  
 •

  
Looks for mode keywords: "server", "client",
      "mirror", "acds", etc.

  
 •

  
Checks for session string pattern (word-word-word) → client
    mode

  
 •

  
Falls back to showing help if no mode specified

  
2.

  
Binary-Level Parsing: Processes global options before mode
      detection:

  
 •

  
--help / -h → prints help and exits

  
 •

  
--version / -v → prints version and exits

  
 •

  
--verbose / -V → sets verbose level (stackable)

  
 •

  
--quiet / -q → sets quiet mode

  
 •

  
--log-file / -L → redirects logs to file

  
 •

  
--log-level → sets log verbosity level

  
3.

  
Mode-Specific Parsing: Uses mode-specific parser for detected
    mode:

  
 •

  
Parses mode-specific options (server, client, mirror, acds)

  
 •

  
Each mode has different set of supported options

  
 •

  
Options are validated with mode bitmasks

  
4.

  
Configuration File Loading (if --config specified):

  
 •

  
Loads TOML config file

  
 •

  
Merges config file settings with command-line options

  
 •

  
Command-line takes precedence over config file

  
5.

  
Validation and Defaults:

  
 •

  
Applies default values to unspecified options

  
 •

  
Validates numeric ranges, file existence, formats

  
 •

  
Performs cross-field validation (dependencies, conflicts)

  
 •

  
Checks mode-specific constraints

  
6.

  
RCU Publishing:

  
 •

  
Initializes RCU state if not already done

  
 •

  
Publishes options struct for lock-free thread-safe access

  
 •

  
After this point, GET_OPTION() and options_get()
      are valid




Detected Mode Storage: The detected mode is stored in
    options_t->detected_mode and accessible via:




asciichat_mode_t mode = GET_OPTION(detected_mode);



Mode Detection Examples:




# Mode keywords (explicit)
./ascii-chat server --port 8080        # MODE_SERVER
./ascii-chat client example.com:9000   # MODE_CLIENT
./ascii-chat mirror --color            # MODE_MIRROR
./ascii-chat acds --port 27225         # MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE
# Session strings (implicit client mode)
./ascii-chat word-word-word            # MODE_CLIENT (session string)
# Help/version (special handling)
./ascii-chat --help                    # Shows help, may exit(0)
./ascii-chat --version                 # Shows version, may exit(0)



Return Values:


    
  
  • ASCIICHAT_OK: Parsing succeeded normally
    • 
  
  • ERROR_USAGE: Parse error occurred (usage info printed to
      stderr)
    • 
  
  • Other error codes: Unexpected errors during initialization
    • 



Special Handling for --help and --version: These flags are
    handled specially and may cause early exit via exit(0). The function will
    still return ASCIICHAT_OK in these cases for compatibility.




Environment Variables: Some options can fall back to
    environment variables:


    
  
  • WEBCAM_DISABLED: Enable test pattern when set to "1",
      "true", etc.
    • 
  
  • ASCII_CHAT_KEY_PASSWORD: Fallback for encrypted key passphrases
    • 
  
  • SSH_AUTH_SOCK: For SSH agent key access
    • 



Typical Usage:




int main(int argc, char **argv) {


    asciichat_error_t err = options_init(argc, argv);


    if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {


        // Could be ERROR_USAGE (invalid options) or other errors


        // usage() already printed to stderr for ERROR_USAGE


        return 1;


    }


    // Options now available via GET_OPTION() and options_get()


    asciichat_mode_t mode = GET_OPTION(detected_mode);


    const char *addr = GET_OPTION(address);


    // ... application code ...


    return 0;
}



Note


Must be called once at program startup before accessing
  options



Should be called before creating worker threads




Global option variables initialized by this function




Returns ERROR_USAGE for invalid options (usage already
  printed)




--help and --version may exit directly via exit(0)




After return, options accessible via GET_OPTION() macro




Initialize all command-line options from argv and environment
    variables




Main entry point for the options parsing system. This
    function:


    
  
  • Detects the mode from the first positional argument (server, client,
      mirror, etc.)
    • 
  
  • Parses binary-level options (--help, --version, --log-file, etc.)
    • 
  
  • Parses mode-specific options
    • 
  
  • Validates cross-field option dependencies
    • 
  
  • Initializes defaults from OPT_*_DEFAULT defines
    • 
  
  • Publishes options via RCU for thread-safe lock-free access
    • 



Option Processing Order:



  
1.

  
Binary-level options (parsed from all args regardless of mode)

  
2.

  
Mode detection (from first positional argument)

  
3.

  
Mode-specific options (from remaining args filtered by mode)

  
4.

  
Defaults initialization (for unspecified options)

  
5.

  
Cross-field validation (dependencies between options)

  
6.

  
RCU publication (make available to all threads)




Return Values:


    
  
  • ASCIICHAT_OK - Initialization successful
    • 
  
  • ERROR_USAGE - Invalid usage (help/usage already printed to stdout)
    • 
  
  • Other errors - Check errno context via HAS_ERRNO()
    • 



Special Behavior:


    
  
  • --help causes help text to print and exit(0) (never returns)
    • 
  
  • --version causes version to print and exit(0) (never returns)
    • 



Parameters


argc Argument count from main()


argv Argument vector from main()


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, ERROR_USAGE on parse error,
  other on fatal error


Note


Global option variables initialized by this function



Returns ERROR_USAGE for invalid options (usage already
  printed)




--help and --version may exit directly via exit(0)




After return, options accessible via GET_OPTION() macro




Definition at line 848 of file
    lib/options/options.c.


848                                                       {
849   log_debug("options_init: starting with argc=%d, argv[0]=%s, argv[1]=%s", argc, argv[0], argc > 1 ? argv[1] : "N/A");
850   // NOTE: --grep filter is initialized in main.c BEFORE any logging starts
851   // This allows ALL logs (including from shared_init) to be filtered
852   // Validate arguments (safety check for tests)
853   if (argc < 0 || argc > 128) {
854     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid argc: %d", argc);
855   }
856   if (argv == NULL) {
857     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "argv is NULL");
858   }
859   // Validate all argv elements are non-NULL up to argc
860   for (int i = 0; i < argc; i++) {
861     if (argv[i] == NULL) {
862       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "argv[%d] is NULL (argc=%d)", i, argc);
863     }
864   }
865   // Initialize RCU options system (must be done before any threads start)
866   // This must happen FIRST, before any other initialization
867   asciichat_error_t rcu_init_result = options_state_init();
868   if (rcu_init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
869     return rcu_init_result;
870   }
871 
872   // ========================================================================
873   // STAGE 1: Mode Detection and Binary-Level Option Handling
874   // ========================================================================
875 
876   // Check for binary-level actions FIRST (before mode detection)
877   // These actions may take arguments, so we need to check them before mode detection
878   bool show_version = false;
879   bool create_config = false;
880   bool create_manpage = false;
881   bool has_action = false; // Track if any action flag is present
882   const char *config_create_path = NULL;
883   const char *manpage_create_path = NULL;
884 
885   // ========================================================================
886   // STAGE 1A: Quick scan for action flags FIRST (they bypass mode detection)
887   // ========================================================================
888   // Quick scan for action flags (they may have arguments)
889   // This must happen BEFORE logging initialization so we can suppress logs before shared_init()
890   // Also scan for --quiet / -q so we can suppress logging from the start
891   // Also scan for --color early so it affects help output colors
892   bool user_quiet = false;
893   int parsed_color_setting = COLOR_SETTING_AUTO; // Store parsed color value until opts is created
894   bool color_setting_found = false;
895   bool check_update_flag_seen = false;
896   bool no_check_update_flag_seen = false;
897 
898   // FIRST: Scan entire argv for --help (special case - works before OR after mode)
899   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
900     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--help") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "-h") == 0) {
901       // Scan backwards to find if a mode was specified before --help
902       asciichat_mode_t help_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY; // Default to discovery mode (binary-level help)
903       for (int j = i - 1; j >= 1; j--) {
904         if (argv[j][0] != '-') {
905           if (strcmp(argv[j], "server") == 0) {
906             help_mode = MODE_SERVER;
907           } else if (strcmp(argv[j], "client") == 0) {
908             help_mode = MODE_CLIENT;
909           } else if (strcmp(argv[j], "mirror") == 0) {
910             help_mode = MODE_MIRROR;
911           } else if (strcmp(argv[j], "discovery-service") == 0) {
912             help_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE;
913           } else if (strcmp(argv[j], "discovery") == 0) {
914             help_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY;
915           }
916           break; // Found first non-flag argument
917         }
918       }
919 
920       // Show help for the detected mode (or binary-level if no mode)
921       usage(stdout, help_mode);
922       fflush(NULL);
923       _Exit(0);
924     }
925   }
926 
927   // THEN: Scan for other binary-level actions (stops at mode name)
928   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
929     // Stop scanning at mode name - binary-level options must come before the mode
930     if (argv[i][0] != '-') {
931       bool is_mode =
932           (strcmp(argv[i], "server") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "client") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "mirror") == 0 ||
933            strcmp(argv[i], "discovery") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "discovery-service") == 0);
934       if (is_mode) {
935         break; // Stop processing at mode name
936       }
937     }
938     if (argv[i][0] == '-') {
939       // Handle --quiet, -q, --quiet=value formats
940       bool temp_quiet = false;
941       if (parse_binary_bool_arg(argv[i], &temp_quiet, "quiet", 'q')) {
942         if (temp_quiet) {
943           user_quiet = true;
944         }
945       }
946       // Validate --log-level and --log-file require arguments
947       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-level") == 0) {
948         if (i + 1 >= argc || argv[i + 1][0] == '-') {
949           log_error("--log-level requires a value (dev, debug, info, warn, error, fatal)");
950           return ERROR_USAGE;
951         }
952         i++; // Skip the argument in this loop
953       }
954       if (strcmp(argv[i], "-L") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "--log-file") == 0) {
955         if (i + 1 >= argc || argv[i + 1][0] == '-') {
956           log_error("%s requires a file path", argv[i]);
957           return ERROR_USAGE;
958         }
959         i++; // Skip the argument in this loop
960       }
961       // --help is handled in the first scan loop above (works before OR after mode)
962       // Parse --color early so it affects help output colors
963       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--color") == 0) {
964         if (i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
965           // Check if next arg is a mode name (not a color value)
966           const char *next_arg = argv[i + 1];
967           bool is_mode =
968               (strcmp(next_arg, "server") == 0 || strcmp(next_arg, "client") == 0 || strcmp(next_arg, "mirror") == 0 ||
969                strcmp(next_arg, "discovery") == 0 || strcmp(next_arg, "discovery-service") == 0);
970 
971           if (is_mode) {
972             // Next arg is a mode, not a color value - default to true
973             parsed_color_setting = COLOR_SETTING_TRUE;
974             color_setting_found = true;
975           } else {
976             // Try to parse as color value
977             char *error_msg = NULL;
978             if (parse_color_setting(next_arg, &parsed_color_setting, &error_msg)) {
979               color_setting_found = true;
980               i++; // Skip the setting argument
981             } else {
982               if (error_msg) {
983                 log_error("Error parsing --color: %s", error_msg);
984                 free(error_msg);
985               }
986             }
987           }
988         } else {
989           // --color without argument defaults to true (enable colors)
990           parsed_color_setting = COLOR_SETTING_TRUE;
991           color_setting_found = true;
992         }
993       }
994       // Check for --color=value format
995       if (strncmp(argv[i], "--color=", 8) == 0) {
996         const char *value = argv[i] + 8;
997         char *error_msg = NULL;
998         if (parse_color_setting(value, &parsed_color_setting, &error_msg)) {
999           color_setting_found = true;
1000         } else {
1001           if (error_msg) {
1002             log_error("Error parsing --color: %s", error_msg);
1003             free(error_msg);
1004           }
1005         }
1006       }
1007       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--version") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "-v") == 0) {
1008         show_version = true;
1009         has_action = true;
1010         break;
1011       }
1012       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--check-update") == 0) {
1013         check_update_flag_seen = true;
1014         if (no_check_update_flag_seen) {
1015           log_error("Cannot specify both --check-update and --no-check-update");
1016           return ERROR_USAGE;
1017         }
1018         has_action = true;
1019         action_check_update_immediate();
1020         // action_check_update_immediate() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1021         break;
1022       }
1023       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--no-check-update") == 0) {
1024         no_check_update_flag_seen = true;
1025         if (check_update_flag_seen) {
1026           log_error("Cannot specify both --check-update and --no-check-update");
1027           return ERROR_USAGE;
1028         }
1029         // Flag will be parsed normally later
1030       }
1031       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--config-create") == 0) {
1032         create_config = true;
1033         has_action = true;
1034         // Check for optional [FILE] argument
1035         if (i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1036           config_create_path = argv[i + 1];
1037           i++; // Consume the file path argument
1038         }
1039         break;
1040       }
1041       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--man-page-create") == 0) {
1042         create_manpage = true;
1043         has_action = true;
1044         // Check for optional [FILE] argument
1045         if (i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1046           manpage_create_path = argv[i + 1];
1047           i++; // Consume the file path argument
1048         }
1049         break;
1050       }
1051       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--completions") == 0) {
1052         has_action = true;
1053         // Handle --completions: generate shell completion scripts
1054         if (i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1055           const char *shell_name = argv[i + 1];
1056           i++; // Consume shell name
1057 
1058           // Check for optional output file
1059           const char *output_file = NULL;
1060           if (i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1061             output_file = argv[i + 1];
1062             i++; // Consume output file
1063           }
1064 
1065           action_completions(shell_name, output_file);
1066           // action_completions() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1067         } else {
1068           log_error("--completions requires shell name (bash, fish, zsh, powershell)");
1069           return ERROR_USAGE;
1070         }
1071         break; // Unreachable, but for clarity
1072       }
1073       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--list-webcams") == 0) {
1074         has_action = true;
1075         action_list_webcams();
1076         // action_list_webcams() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1077         break;
1078       }
1079       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--list-microphones") == 0) {
1080         has_action = true;
1081         action_list_microphones();
1082         // action_list_microphones() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1083         break;
1084       }
1085       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--list-speakers") == 0) {
1086         has_action = true;
1087         action_list_speakers();
1088         // action_list_speakers() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1089         break;
1090       }
1091       // Check for --show-capabilities (binary-level action)
1092       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--show-capabilities") == 0) {
1093         has_action = true;
1094         action_show_capabilities_immediate();
1095         // action_show_capabilities_immediate() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1096         break;
1097       }
1098     }
1099   }
1100   // Store action flag globally for use during cleanup
1101   set_action_flag(has_action);
1102   // ========================================================================
1103   // STAGE 1B: DO MODE DETECTION EARLY (needed for log_init)
1104   // ========================================================================
1105   asciichat_mode_t detected_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY; // Default mode
1106   char detected_session_string[SESSION_STRING_BUFFER_SIZE] = {0};
1107   int mode_index = -1;
1108 
1109   asciichat_error_t mode_detect_result =
1110       options_detect_mode(argc, argv, &detected_mode, detected_session_string, &mode_index);
1111   if (mode_detect_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1112     return mode_detect_result;
1113   }
1114 
1115   // VALIDATE: Binary-level options must appear BEFORE the mode
1116   // Check if any binary-level options appear after the mode position
1117   if (mode_index > 0) {
1118     for (int i = mode_index + 1; i < argc; i++) {
1119       if (argv[i][0] == '-') {
1120         bool takes_arg = false;
1121         bool takes_optional_arg = false;
1122 
1123         if (is_binary_level_option_with_args(argv[i], &takes_arg, &takes_optional_arg)) {
1124           return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_USAGE, "Binary-level option '%s' must appear before the mode '%s', not after it",
1125                            argv[i], argv[mode_index]);
1126         }
1127       }
1128     }
1129   }
1130   // ========================================================================
1131   // STAGE 1C: Initialize logging EARLY (before any log_dev calls)
1132   // ========================================================================
1133   // Create local options struct and initialize with defaults
1134   options_t opts = options_t_new(); // Initialize with all defaults
1135   opts.detected_mode = detected_mode;
1136   char *log_filename = options_get_log_filepath(detected_mode, opts);
1137   SAFE_SNPRINTF(opts.log_file, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", log_filename);
1138   // Note: log_init() is called earlier in asciichat_shared_init() and will be
1139   // reconfigured with the actual log level and file in main.c after options are fully parsed
1140   // NOTE: --color detection now happens in src/main.c BEFORE asciichat_shared_init()
1141   // This ensures g_color_flag_passed and g_color_flag_value are set before any logging.
1142   //
1143   // NOTE: Timer system and shared subsystems are initialized by src/main.c
1144   // via asciichat_shared_init() BEFORE options_init() is called.
1145   // This allows options_init() to use properly configured logging.
1146 
1147   // If an action flag is detected OR user passed --quiet, silence logs for clean output
1148   if (user_quiet || has_action) {
1149     log_set_terminal_output(false); // Suppress console logging for clean action output
1150   }
1151   // Apply parsed color setting from STAGE 1A
1152   if (color_setting_found) {
1153     opts.color = parsed_color_setting;
1154   } else {
1155     opts.color = OPT_COLOR_DEFAULT; // Apply default
1156   }
1157 
1158   // If we found version/config-create/create-manpage, handle them immediately (before mode detection)
1159   if (show_version || create_config || create_manpage) {
1160     if (show_version) {
1161       opts.version = true;
1162       options_state_set(&opts);
1163       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1164     } else if (create_config) {
1165       // Build the schema first so config_create_default can generate options from it
1166       const options_config_t *unified_config = options_preset_unified(NULL, NULL);
1167       if (unified_config) {
1168         asciichat_error_t schema_build_result = config_schema_build_from_configs(&unified_config, 1);
1169         if (schema_build_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1170           // Schema build failed, but continue anyway
1171           (void)schema_build_result;
1172         }
1173         options_config_destroy(unified_config);
1174       }
1175 
1176       // Call action handler which handles all output and prompts properly
1177       action_create_config(config_create_path);
1178       // action_create_config() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1179     } else if (create_manpage) {
1180       // Call action handler which handles all output and prompts properly
1181       action_create_manpage(manpage_create_path);
1182       // action_create_manpage() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
1183     }
1184   }
1185 
1186   // Mode detection and logging already initialized early in STAGE 1B/1C above
1187   // (moved earlier to ensure logging is available before any log_dev() calls)
1188 
1189   // Check for binary-level options that can appear before or after mode
1190   // Search entire argv to find --quiet, --log-file, --log-level, -V, etc.
1191   // These are documented as binary-level options that can appear anywhere
1192   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
1193     if (argv[i][0] == '-') {
1194       // Handle -V and --verbose (stackable verbosity)
1195       if (strcmp(argv[i], "-V") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "--verbose") == 0) {
1196         // Check if next argument is a number (optional argument)
1197         if (i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1198           // Try to parse as integer count
1199           char *endptr;
1200           long value = strtol(argv[i + 1], &endptr, 10);
1201           if (*endptr == '\0' && value >= 0 && value <= 100) {
1202             opts.verbose_level = (unsigned short int)value;
1203             i++; // Skip next argument
1204             continue;
1205           }
1206         }
1207         // No valid number, just increment
1208         opts.verbose_level++;
1209       }
1210       // Handle binary-level boolean options using the abstraction function
1211       // -q/--quiet, --json, --log-format-console all use the same parser
1212       if (parse_binary_bool_arg(argv[i], &opts.quiet, "quiet", 'q')) {
1213         continue;
1214       }
1215       if (parse_binary_bool_arg(argv[i], &opts.json, "json", '\0')) {
1216         continue;
1217       }
1218       if (parse_binary_bool_arg(argv[i], &opts.log_format_console_only, "log-format-console", '\0')) {
1219         continue;
1220       }
1221       // Handle --log-level LEVEL (set log threshold)
1222       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-level") == 0) {
1223         if (i + 1 >= argc) {
1224           log_error("--log-level requires a value (dev, debug, info, warn, error, fatal)");
1225           return ERROR_USAGE;
1226         }
1227         if (argv[i + 1][0] == '-') {
1228           log_error("--log-level requires a value (dev, debug, info, warn, error, fatal)");
1229           return ERROR_USAGE;
1230         }
1231         char *error_msg = NULL;
1232         if (parse_log_level(argv[i + 1], &opts.log_level, &error_msg)) {
1233           i++; // Skip the level argument
1234         } else {
1235           if (error_msg) {
1236             log_error("%s", error_msg);
1237             free(error_msg);
1238           } else {
1239             log_error("invalid log level value: %s", argv[i + 1]);
1240           }
1241           return ERROR_USAGE;
1242         }
1243       }
1244       // Handle -L and --log-file FILE (set log file path)
1245       if ((strcmp(argv[i], "-L") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "--log-file") == 0)) {
1246         if (i + 1 >= argc) {
1247           log_error("%s requires a file path", argv[i]);
1248           return ERROR_USAGE;
1249         }
1250         if (argv[i + 1][0] == '-') {
1251           log_error("%s requires a file path", argv[i]);
1252           return ERROR_USAGE;
1253         }
1254         SAFE_STRNCPY(opts.log_file, argv[i + 1], sizeof(opts.log_file));
1255         i++; // Skip the file argument
1256       }
1257       // NOTE: --color is now parsed in STAGE 1A (early, before help processing)
1258       // to ensure help output colors are applied correctly
1259     }
1260   }
1261 
1262   if (show_version) {
1263     // Show binary-level version from src/main.c
1264     opts.version = true;
1265     options_state_set(&opts);
1266     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1267   }
1268 
1269   if (create_config) {
1270     // Handle --config-create: create default config file and exit
1271     // Use provided path or default to user config location
1272     char config_path[PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH];
1273     if (config_create_path) {
1274       SAFE_STRNCPY(config_path, config_create_path, sizeof(config_path));
1275     } else {
1276       // Use default config path: ~/.ascii-chat/config.toml
1277       char *config_dir = get_config_dir();
1278       if (!config_dir) {
1279         log_error("Error: Failed to determine default config directory");
1280         return ERROR_CONFIG;
1281       }
1282       safe_snprintf(config_path, sizeof(config_path), "%sconfig.toml", config_dir);
1283       SAFE_FREE(config_dir);
1284     }
1285 
1286     // Create config with default options
1287     asciichat_error_t result = config_create_default(config_path);
1288     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1289       asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1290       if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1291         log_error("Error creating config: %s", err_ctx.context_message);
1292       } else {
1293         log_error("Error: Failed to create config file at %s", config_path);
1294       }
1295       return result;
1296     }
1297 
1298     log_plain("Created default config file at: %s", config_path);
1299     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return successfully after creating config
1300   }
1301 
1302   if (create_manpage) {
1303     // Handle --create-man-page-template: generate merged man page template
1304     // The .1.in file is the existing template to read from (not the output)
1305     const char *existing_template_path = "share/man/man1/ascii-chat.1.in";
1306     options_config_t *config = options_preset_unified(NULL, NULL);
1307     if (!config) {
1308       log_error("Error: Failed to get binary options config");
1309       return ERROR_MEMORY;
1310     }
1311 
1312     // Generate merged man page from embedded or filesystem resources
1313     // (existing_template_path and manpage_content_file parameters are no longer supported)
1314     asciichat_error_t err = options_config_generate_manpage_merged(config, "ascii-chat", NULL, existing_template_path,
1315                                                                    "Video chat in your terminal");
1316 
1317     options_config_destroy(config);
1318 
1319     if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1320       asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1321       if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1322         log_error("%s", err_ctx.context_message);
1323       } else {
1324         log_error("Error: Failed to generate man page");
1325       }
1326       return err;
1327     }
1328 
1329     log_plain("Generated man page: %s", existing_template_path);
1330     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return successfully after generating man page
1331   }
1332 
1333   // ========================================================================
1334   // STAGE 2: Build argv for mode-specific parsing
1335   // ========================================================================
1336   // If mode was found, build argv with only arguments after the mode
1337   // If mode_index == -1, filter out binary-level options from all arguments
1338   int mode_argc = argc;
1339   char **mode_argv = (char **)argv;
1340   char **allocated_mode_argv = NULL; // Track if we need to free mode_argv
1341 
1342   if (mode_index == -1) {
1343     // No explicit mode - filter binary-level options from entire argv
1344     int max_mode_argc = argc; // Worst case: no binary opts skipped
1345 
1346     if (max_mode_argc > 256) {
1347       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Too many arguments: %d", max_mode_argc);
1348     }
1349 
1350     char **new_mode_argv = SAFE_MALLOC((size_t)(max_mode_argc + 1) * sizeof(char *), char **);
1351     if (!new_mode_argv) {
1352       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate mode_argv");
1353     }
1354     allocated_mode_argv = new_mode_argv;
1355 
1356     new_mode_argv[0] = argv[0]; // Copy program name
1357 
1358     // Copy all args except binary-level options
1359     int new_argv_idx = 1;
1360     for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
1361       bool takes_arg = false;
1362       bool takes_optional_arg = false;
1363 
1364       // Check if this is a binary-level option
1365       if (is_binary_level_option_with_args(argv[i], &takes_arg, &takes_optional_arg)) {
1366         // Skip argument if needed
1367         // NOLINTNEXTLINE(bugprone-branch-clone)
1368         if (takes_arg && i + 1 < argc) {
1369           i++; // Skip required argument
1370         } else if (takes_optional_arg && i + 1 < argc && argv[i + 1][0] != '-') {
1371           i++; // Skip optional argument
1372         }
1373         continue;
1374       }
1375       // Not a binary option, copy to mode_argv
1376       new_mode_argv[new_argv_idx++] = argv[i];
1377     }
1378 
1379     mode_argc = new_argv_idx;
1380     new_mode_argv[mode_argc] = NULL;
1381     mode_argv = new_mode_argv;
1382   } else if (mode_index != -1) {
1383     // Mode found at position mode_index
1384     // Build new argv: [program_name, args_before_mode (no binary opts)..., args_after_mode...]
1385     // Binary-level options are not passed to mode-specific parsers
1386 
1387     int args_after_mode = argc - mode_index - 1;
1388     // We'll calculate final argc after skipping binary options
1389     int max_mode_argc = 1 + (mode_index - 1) + args_after_mode; // Worst case: no binary opts skipped
1390 
1391     if (max_mode_argc > 256) {
1392       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Too many arguments: %d", max_mode_argc);
1393     }
1394 
1395     // Allocate max_mode_argc+1 to accommodate NULL terminator
1396     char **new_mode_argv = SAFE_MALLOC((size_t)(max_mode_argc + 1) * sizeof(char *), char **);
1397     if (!new_mode_argv) {
1398       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate mode_argv");
1399     }
1400     allocated_mode_argv = new_mode_argv; // Track allocation for cleanup
1401 
1402     // Copy: [program_name, args_before_mode (excluding binary opts)..., args_after_mode...]
1403     if (mode_index == 0) {
1404       // Mode is at argv[0], use "ascii-chat" as program name
1405       new_mode_argv[0] = "ascii-chat";
1406     } else {
1407       new_mode_argv[0] = argv[0];
1408     }
1409 
1410     // Copy args before mode, skipping binary-level options
1411     int new_argv_idx = 1;
1412     for (int i = 1; i < mode_index; i++) {
1413       bool takes_arg = false;
1414       bool takes_optional_arg = false;
1415 
1416       // Check if this is a binary-level option
1417       if (is_binary_level_option_with_args(argv[i], &takes_arg, &takes_optional_arg)) {
1418         // Skip argument if needed
1419         if ((takes_arg || (takes_optional_arg && argv[i + 1][0] != '-')) && i + 1 < mode_index) {
1420           i++; // Skip argument
1421         }
1422         continue;
1423       }
1424       // Not a binary option, copy to mode_argv
1425       new_mode_argv[new_argv_idx++] = argv[i];
1426     }
1427     // Copy args after mode, filtering out any binary-level options (they shouldn't appear here)
1428     int args_after_mode_idx = 0;
1429     for (int i = mode_index + 1; i < argc; i++) {
1430       bool takes_arg = false;
1431       bool takes_optional_arg = false;
1432 
1433       // Check if this is a binary-level option (shouldn't appear after mode)
1434       if (is_binary_level_option_with_args(argv[i], &takes_arg, &takes_optional_arg)) {
1435         // Skip binary-level option and its argument if needed
1436         if ((takes_arg || (takes_optional_arg && argv[i + 1][0] != '-')) && i + 1 < argc) {
1437           i++; // Skip argument
1438         }
1439         continue; // Skip this option
1440       }
1441       // Not a binary option, copy to mode_argv
1442       new_mode_argv[new_argv_idx + args_after_mode_idx++] = argv[i];
1443     }
1444     // Calculate actual argc (program + filtered args before + filtered args after)
1445     mode_argc = new_argv_idx + args_after_mode_idx;
1446     new_mode_argv[mode_argc] = NULL;
1447 
1448     mode_argv = new_mode_argv;
1449   }
1450 
1451   // ========================================================================
1452   // STAGE 3: Set Mode-Specific Defaults
1453   // ========================================================================
1454   // Initialize all defaults using options_t_new_preserve_binary() to keep binary-level
1455   // options (--quiet, --verbose, --log-level, etc.) from being reset
1456   opts = options_t_new_preserve_binary(&opts);
1457   // If a session string was detected during mode detection, restore it after options reset
1458   // This ensures discovery mode knows which session to join
1459   if (detected_session_string[0] != '\0') {
1460     SAFE_STRNCPY(opts.session_string, detected_session_string, sizeof(opts.session_string));
1461     log_info("options_init: Detected session string from argv: '%s'", detected_session_string);
1462   }
1463 
1464   // Encryption options default to disabled/empty
1465   opts.no_encrypt = 0;
1466   opts.encrypt_key[0] = '\0';
1467   opts.password[0] = '\0';
1468   opts.encrypt_keyfile[0] = '\0';
1469   opts.server_key[0] = '\0';
1470   opts.client_keys[0] = '\0';
1471   opts.palette_custom[0] = '\0';
1472 
1473   // Set different default addresses for different modes
1474   if (detected_mode == MODE_CLIENT || detected_mode == MODE_MIRROR || detected_mode == MODE_DISCOVERY) {
1475     // Client/Mirror/Discovery: connects to localhost by default (discovery uses ACDS to find server)
1476     SAFE_SNPRINTF(opts.address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "localhost");
1477     opts.address6[0] = '\0'; // Client doesn't use address6
1478   } else if (detected_mode == MODE_SERVER) {
1479     // Server: binds to 127.0.0.1 (IPv4) and ::1 (IPv6) by default
1480     SAFE_SNPRINTF(opts.address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "127.0.0.1");
1481     // address6 is now a positional argument, not an option
1482     opts.address6[0] = '\0';
1483   } else if (detected_mode == MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE) {
1484     // ACDS: binds to all interfaces by default
1485     SAFE_SNPRINTF(opts.address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "0.0.0.0");
1486     opts.address6[0] = '\0';
1487   }
1488 
1489   // ========================================================================
1490   // STAGE 4: Build Dynamic Schema from Unified Options Config
1491   // ========================================================================
1492   // Build the config schema dynamically from the unified config
1493   // This generates TOML keys, CLI flags, categories, and types from builder data
1494   const options_config_t *unified_config = options_preset_unified(NULL, NULL);
1495   if (unified_config) {
1496     asciichat_error_t schema_build_result = config_schema_build_from_configs(&unified_config, 1);
1497     if (schema_build_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1498       // Schema build failed, but continue with static schema as fallback
1499       (void)schema_build_result;
1500     } else {
1501     }
1502     options_config_destroy(unified_config);
1503   } else {
1504   }
1505 
1506   // ========================================================================
1507   // STAGE 5: Load Configuration Files
1508   // ========================================================================
1509   // Extract binary-level options BEFORE config loading (config may reset them)
1510   binary_level_opts_t binary_before_config = extract_binary_level(&opts);
1511   // Publish options to RCU before config loading so that config logs get proper colors
1512   // from the parsed --color setting (e.g., --color=true)
1513   asciichat_error_t config_publish_result = options_state_set(&opts);
1514   (void)config_publish_result; // Suppress unused variable warning
1515   // Load config files - now uses detected_mode directly for bitmask validation
1516   // Save flip_x and flip_y as they should not be reset by config files
1517   // Also save encryption settings - they should only be controlled via CLI, not config file
1518   bool saved_flip_x_from_config = opts.flip_x;
1519   bool saved_flip_y_from_config = opts.flip_y;
1520   bool saved_encrypt_enabled = opts.encrypt_enabled;
1521   asciichat_error_t config_result = config_load_system_and_user(detected_mode, false, &opts);
1522   (void)config_result; // Continue with defaults and CLI parsing regardless of result
1523   // Restore binary-level options (don't let config override command-line options)
1524   restore_binary_level(&opts, &binary_before_config);
1525 
1526   // Restore flip_x and flip_y - config shouldn't override the defaults
1527   opts.flip_x = saved_flip_x_from_config;
1528   opts.flip_y = saved_flip_y_from_config;
1529 
1530   // Restore encrypt_enabled - it should only be set via CLI, not config file
1531   // The config can set key/password which auto-enables encryption, but the encrypt_enabled
1532   // flag itself should stay at its default unless the user explicitly passes --encrypt
1533   opts.encrypt_enabled = saved_encrypt_enabled;
1534 
1535   // ========================================================================
1536   // STAGE 6: Parse Command-Line Arguments (Unified)
1537   // ========================================================================
1538   // Extract binary-level options BEFORE applying unified defaults
1539   // (which might override them from config files)
1540   binary_level_opts_t binary_before_defaults = extract_binary_level(&opts);
1541   asciichat_mode_t mode_saved_for_parsing = detected_mode; // CRITICAL: Save before defaults reset
1542   // Get unified config
1543   options_config_t *config = options_preset_unified(NULL, NULL);
1544   if (!config) {
1545     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1546     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to create options configuration");
1547   }
1548   int remaining_argc;
1549   char **remaining_argv;
1550 
1551   // Save flip_x and flip_y before applying defaults (should not be reset by defaults)
1552   bool saved_flip_x = opts.flip_x;
1553   bool saved_flip_y = opts.flip_y;
1554   // Apply defaults from unified config
1555   asciichat_error_t defaults_result = options_config_set_defaults(config, &opts);
1556   if (defaults_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1557     options_config_destroy(config);
1558     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1559     return defaults_result;
1560   }
1561   // Restore binary-level options (they should never be overridden by defaults)
1562   restore_binary_level(&opts, &binary_before_defaults);
1563 
1564   // Restore flip_x and flip_y - they should keep the values from options_t_new()
1565   // unless explicitly set by the user (but defaults shouldn't override them)
1566   opts.flip_x = saved_flip_x;
1567   opts.flip_y = saved_flip_y;
1568 
1569   // Restore detected_mode before parsing so mode validation works.
1570   opts.detected_mode = mode_saved_for_parsing;
1571 
1572   // Save flip_x and flip_y before parsing - they should not be reset by the parser
1573   bool saved_flip_x_for_parse = opts.flip_x;
1574   bool saved_flip_y_for_parse = opts.flip_y;
1575   // Note: json is already saved via extract_binary_level/restore_binary_level mechanism
1576   // Parse mode-specific arguments
1577   option_mode_bitmask_t mode_bitmask = (1 << mode_saved_for_parsing);
1578   log_debug("About to parse options_config with mode_bitmask=%u, mode_argc=%d", mode_bitmask, mode_argc);
1579   asciichat_error_t result =
1580       options_config_parse(config, mode_argc, mode_argv, &opts, mode_bitmask, &remaining_argc, &remaining_argv);
1581   log_debug("options_config_parse returned: %d", result);
1582   // Restore flip_x and flip_y - they should keep their values unless explicitly overridden
1583   opts.flip_x = saved_flip_x_for_parse;
1584   opts.flip_y = saved_flip_y_for_parse;
1585   // json is already restored via the call to options_state_set which calls restore_binary_level
1586   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1587     options_config_destroy(config);
1588     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1589     // Convert ERROR_CONFIG to ERROR_USAGE for command-line parsing errors
1590     if (result == ERROR_CONFIG) {
1591       asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1592       const char *error_detail = "invalid options";
1593       if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1594         error_detail = err_ctx.context_message;
1595       }
1596       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_USAGE, "Failed to parse options: %s", error_detail);
1597     }
1598     return result;
1599   }
1600 
1601   // ========================================================================
1602   // STAGE 6.5: Publish Parsed Options Early
1603   // ========================================================================
1604   // Publish options to RCU as soon as they're parsed
1605   // This ensures GET_OPTION() works during cleanup even if validation fails
1606   log_debug("Publishing parsed options to RCU before validation");
1607   asciichat_error_t early_publish = options_state_set(&opts);
1608   if (early_publish != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1609     log_error("Failed to publish parsed options to RCU state early");
1610     options_config_destroy(config);
1611     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1612     return early_publish;
1613   }
1614   log_debug("Successfully published options to RCU");
1615 
1616   // Auto-enable custom palette if palette-chars was set
1617   if (opts.palette_custom[0] != '\0') {
1618     // palette-chars was set - always use PALETTE_CUSTOM (overrides any explicit --palette setting)
1619     opts.palette_type = PALETTE_CUSTOM;
1620     opts.palette_custom_set = true;
1621     log_debug("Set PALETTE_CUSTOM because --palette-chars was provided");
1622 
1623     // Validate palette characters for UTF-8 correctness
1624     if (!utf8_is_valid(opts.palette_custom)) {
1625       log_error("Error: --palette-chars contains invalid UTF-8 sequences");
1626       options_config_destroy(config);
1627       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1628       return option_error_invalid();
1629     }
1630 
1631     // Check if palette contains non-ASCII characters
1632     bool has_non_ascii = !utf8_is_ascii_only(opts.palette_custom);
1633     if (has_non_ascii) {
1634       // Non-ASCII characters require UTF-8 support
1635       // Check if UTF-8 is explicitly disabled or unavailable
1636       bool utf8_disabled = (opts.force_utf8 == COLOR_SETTING_FALSE);
1637       bool utf8_auto_unavailable = (opts.force_utf8 == COLOR_SETTING_AUTO && !terminal_supports_utf8());
1638 
1639       if (utf8_disabled) {
1640         log_error("Error: --palette-chars contains non-ASCII characters but --utf8=false was specified");
1641         log_error("       Remove --utf8=false or use ASCII-only palette characters");
1642         options_config_destroy(config);
1643         SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1644         return option_error_invalid();
1645       }
1646 
1647       if (utf8_auto_unavailable) {
1648         log_error("Error: --palette-chars contains non-ASCII characters but terminal does not support UTF-8");
1649         log_error("       Use --utf8=true to force UTF-8 mode or use ASCII-only palette characters");
1650         options_config_destroy(config);
1651         SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1652         return option_error_invalid();
1653       }
1654     }
1655   }
1656 
1657   // Auto-enable encryption if key was provided
1658   if (opts.encrypt_key[0] != '\0') {
1659     // Validate key file exists (skip for remote/virtual keys)
1660     if (!is_remote_key_path(opts.encrypt_key)) {
1661       struct stat st;
1662       if (stat(opts.encrypt_key, &st) != 0) {
1663         log_error("Key file not found: %s", opts.encrypt_key);
1664         options_config_destroy(config);
1665         SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1666         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Key file not found: %s", opts.encrypt_key);
1667       }
1668       if ((st.st_mode & S_IFMT) != S_IFREG) {
1669         log_error("Key path is not a regular file: %s", opts.encrypt_key);
1670         options_config_destroy(config);
1671         SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1672         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_KEY, "Key path is not a regular file: %s", opts.encrypt_key);
1673       }
1674     }
1675     opts.encrypt_enabled = 1;
1676     log_debug("Auto-enabled encryption because --key was provided");
1677   }
1678 
1679   // Color filter validation and auto-enable
1680   if (opts.color_filter != COLOR_FILTER_NONE) {
1681     // Color filter requires color to be enabled
1682     if (opts.color == COLOR_SETTING_FALSE) {
1683       log_error("Error: --color-filter cannot be used with --color=false");
1684       options_config_destroy(config);
1685       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1686       return option_error_invalid();
1687     }
1688     // Auto-enable color when color filter is specified
1689     opts.color = COLOR_SETTING_TRUE;
1690     log_debug("Auto-enabled color because --color-filter was provided");
1691   }
1692 
1693   // Detect if splash or status_screen were explicitly set on command line
1694   for (int i = 0; i < mode_argc; i++) {
1695     if (mode_argv[i] &&
1696         (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--splash-screen") == 0 || strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--splash-screen=", 16) == 0)) {
1697       // Parse the value if provided (--splash-screen=true/false)
1698       bool splash_value = true; // Default to true for plain --splash-screen
1699       if (strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--splash-screen=", 16) == 0) {
1700         const char *value = mode_argv[i] + 16;
1701         if (strcasecmp(value, "false") == 0 || strcasecmp(value, "no") == 0 ||
1702             strcasecmp(value, "0") == 0 || strcasecmp(value, "off") == 0) {
1703           splash_value = false;
1704         }
1705       }
1706       opts.splash_screen = splash_value;
1707       opts.splash_screen_explicitly_set = true;
1708     }
1709     if (mode_argv[i] &&
1710         (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--status-screen") == 0 || strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--status-screen=", 16) == 0)) {
1711       // Parse the value if provided (--status-screen=true/false)
1712       bool status_value = true; // Default to true for plain --status-screen
1713       if (strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--status-screen=", 16) == 0) {
1714         const char *value = mode_argv[i] + 16;
1715         if (strcasecmp(value, "false") == 0 || strcasecmp(value, "no") == 0 ||
1716             strcasecmp(value, "0") == 0 || strcasecmp(value, "off") == 0) {
1717           status_value = false;
1718         }
1719       }
1720       opts.status_screen = status_value;
1721       opts.status_screen_explicitly_set = true;
1722     }
1723 #ifndef NDEBUG
1724     if (mode_argv[i] &&
1725         (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--sync-state") == 0 || strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--sync-state=", 13) == 0)) {
1726       opts.debug_sync_state_time_explicit = true;
1727       if (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--sync-state") == 0) {
1728         if (i + 1 < mode_argc) {
1729           char *endptr;
1730           double val = strtod(mode_argv[i + 1], &endptr);
1731           if (endptr != mode_argv[i + 1] && val > 0.0) {
1732             opts.debug_sync_state_time = val;
1733             i++;
1734           }
1735         }
1736       } else {
1737         const char *value_str = mode_argv[i] + 13;
1738         if (value_str[0] != '\0') {
1739           char *endptr;
1740           double val = strtod(value_str, &endptr);
1741           if (endptr != value_str && val > 0.0) {
1742             opts.debug_sync_state_time = val;
1743           }
1744         }
1745       }
1746     }
1747     if (mode_argv[i] && (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--backtrace") == 0 || strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--backtrace=", 12) == 0)) {
1748       opts.debug_backtrace_time_explicit = true;
1749       if (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--backtrace") == 0) {
1750         if (i + 1 < mode_argc) {
1751           char *endptr;
1752           double val = strtod(mode_argv[i + 1], &endptr);
1753           if (endptr != mode_argv[i + 1] && val > 0.0) {
1754             opts.debug_backtrace_time = val;
1755             i++;
1756           }
1757         }
1758       } else {
1759         const char *value_str = mode_argv[i] + 12;
1760         if (value_str[0] != '\0') {
1761           char *endptr;
1762           double val = strtod(value_str, &endptr);
1763           if (endptr != value_str && val > 0.0) {
1764             opts.debug_backtrace_time = val;
1765           }
1766         }
1767       }
1768     }
1769     if (mode_argv[i] &&
1770         (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--memory-report") == 0 || strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--memory-report=", 16) == 0)) {
1771       opts.debug_memory_report_interval_explicit = true;
1772       if (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--memory-report") == 0) {
1773         if (i + 1 < mode_argc) {
1774           char *endptr;
1775           double val = strtod(mode_argv[i + 1], &endptr);
1776           if (endptr != mode_argv[i + 1] && val > 0.0) {
1777             opts.debug_memory_report_interval = val;
1778             i++;
1779           }
1780         }
1781       } else {
1782         const char *value_str = mode_argv[i] + 16;
1783         if (value_str[0] != '\0') {
1784           char *endptr;
1785           double val = strtod(value_str, &endptr);
1786           if (endptr != value_str && val > 0.0) {
1787             opts.debug_memory_report_interval = val;
1788           }
1789         }
1790       }
1791     }
1792 #endif
1793   }
1794 
1795   // Auto-disable splash when grep is used (since it's one-time startup screen)
1796   // UNLESS it was explicitly set by the user
1797   // Status screen is now compatible with --grep since we support auto-loading patterns
1798   bool grep_was_provided = false;
1799   for (int i = 0; i < mode_argc; i++) {
1800     if (mode_argv[i] && (strcmp(mode_argv[i], "--grep") == 0 || strncmp(mode_argv[i], "--grep=", 7) == 0)) {
1801       grep_was_provided = true;
1802       break;
1803     }
1804   }
1805 
1806   if (grep_was_provided) {
1807     if (!opts.splash_screen_explicitly_set) {
1808       opts.splash_screen = false;
1809       log_debug("Auto-disabled splash because --grep was provided");
1810     }
1811   }
1812 
1813   // Auto-disable splash and status screens when terminal is non-interactive or CLAUDECODE is set
1814   // This ensures clean output when running under LLM automation or non-interactive shells
1815   bool is_terminal_interactive = terminal_is_interactive();
1816   bool is_claudecode_set = platform_getenv("CLAUDECODE") != NULL;
1817   bool should_auto_disable_screens = !is_terminal_interactive || is_claudecode_set;
1818 
1819   if (should_auto_disable_screens) {
1820     if (!opts.splash_screen_explicitly_set) {
1821       opts.splash_screen = false;
1822       log_debug("Auto-disabled splash (non-interactive terminal or CLAUDECODE set)");
1823     }
1824     if (!opts.status_screen_explicitly_set) {
1825       opts.status_screen = false;
1826       log_debug("Auto-disabled status screen (non-interactive terminal or CLAUDECODE set)");
1827     }
1828   }
1829 
1830   // Validate all string options contain valid UTF-8
1831   // This prevents crashes and corruption from invalid UTF-8 in any option
1832   // DISABLED IN RELEASE BUILDS: utf8_is_valid() has performance issues with musl
1833   #ifndef NDEBUG
1834   const char *string_fields[][2] = {{"address", opts.address},
1835                                     {"address6", opts.address6},
1836                                     {"encrypt_key", opts.encrypt_key},
1837                                     {"encrypt_keyfile", opts.encrypt_keyfile},
1838                                     {"server_key", opts.server_key},
1839                                     {"client_keys", opts.client_keys},
1840                                     {"discovery_server", opts.discovery_server},
1841                                     {"discovery_service_key", opts.discovery_service_key},
1842                                     {"discovery_database_path", opts.discovery_database_path},
1843                                     {"log_file", opts.log_file},
1844                                     {"media_file", opts.media_file},
1845                                     {"palette_custom", opts.palette_custom},
1846                                     {"stun_servers", opts.stun_servers},
1847                                     {"turn_servers", opts.turn_servers},
1848                                     {"turn_username", opts.turn_username},
1849                                     {"turn_credential", opts.turn_credential},
1850                                     {"turn_secret", opts.turn_secret},
1851                                     {"session_string", opts.session_string},
1852                                     {NULL, NULL}};
1853 
1854   for (int i = 0; string_fields[i][0] != NULL; i++) {
1855     const char *field_name = string_fields[i][0];
1856     const char *field_value = string_fields[i][1];
1857 
1858     // Skip empty strings
1859     if (!field_value || field_value[0] == '\0') {
1860       continue;
1861     }
1862 
1863     // Validate UTF-8
1864     if (!utf8_is_valid(field_value)) {
1865       log_error("Error: Option --%s contains invalid UTF-8 sequences", field_name);
1866       log_error("       Value: %s", field_value);
1867       options_config_destroy(config);
1868       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1869       return option_error_invalid();
1870     }
1871   }
1872   #endif
1873   // Validate options
1874   log_info("★ VALIDATE_OPTIONS_AND_REPORT: About to call");
1875   result = validate_options_and_report(config, &opts);
1876   log_info("★ VALIDATE_OPTIONS_AND_REPORT: Returned with result=%d", result);
1877   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1878     options_config_destroy(config);
1879     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1880     return result;
1881   }
1882   // Check for unexpected remaining arguments
1883   if (remaining_argc > 0) {
1884     log_error("Error: Unexpected arguments after options:");
1885     for (int i = 0; i < remaining_argc; i++) {
1886       log_error("  %s", remaining_argv[i]);
1887     }
1888     options_config_destroy(config);
1889     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1890     return option_error_invalid();
1891   }
1892 
1893   // Mode-specific post-processing
1894   // Apply mode-specific defaults (port, websocket-port)
1895   log_info("★ APPLY_MODE_SPECIFIC: About to call");
1896   apply_mode_specific_defaults(&opts);
1897   log_info("★ APPLY_MODE_SPECIFIC: Done");
1898   log_dev("Applied mode-specific defaults: port=%d, websocket_port=%d", opts.port, opts.websocket_port);
1899 
1900   if (detected_mode == MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE) {
1901     // Set default paths if not specified
1902     log_info("★ DISCOVERY_SERVICE: Handling discovery service defaults");
1903     if (opts.discovery_database_path[0] == '\0') {
1904       // Database: Try system-wide location first, fall back to user directories
1905       // Preference: /usr/local/var/ascii-chat/ > ~/.local/share/ascii-chat/ > ~/.config/ascii-chat/
1906       log_info("★ DISCOVERY_SERVICE: About to call get_discovery_database_dir()");
1907       char *db_dir = get_discovery_database_dir();
1908       log_info("★ DISCOVERY_SERVICE: get_discovery_database_dir() returned %p", (void*)db_dir);
1909       if (!db_dir) {
1910         options_config_destroy(config);
1911         SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1912         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to get database directory (tried system and user locations)");
1913       }
1914       safe_snprintf(opts.discovery_database_path, sizeof(opts.discovery_database_path), "%sdiscovery.db", db_dir);
1915       SAFE_FREE(db_dir);
1916     }
1917   }
1918 
1919   log_info("★ OPTIONS_CONFIG_DESTROY: About to call");
1920   options_config_destroy(config);
1921   log_info("★ OPTIONS_CONFIG_DESTROY: Done");
1922 
1923   // ========================================================================
1924   // STAGE 7: Post-Processing & Validation
1925   // ========================================================================
1926 
1927   log_info("★ STAGE 7: Starting post-processing");
1928 
1929   // Collect multiple --key flags for multi-key support (server/ACDS only)
1930   // This enables servers to load both SSH and GPG keys and select the right one
1931   // during handshake based on what the client expects
1932   if (detected_mode == MODE_SERVER || detected_mode == MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE) {
1933     log_info("★ STAGE 7.1: About to collect identity keys");
1934     int num_keys = options_collect_identity_keys(&opts, argc, argv);
1935     log_info("★ STAGE 7.1: options_collect_identity_keys returned %d", num_keys);
1936     if (num_keys < 0) {
1937       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1938       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Failed to collect identity keys");
1939     }
1940     // num_keys == 0 is OK (no --key flags provided)
1941   }
1942 
1943   log_info("★ STAGE 7.2: About to update dimensions");
1944 
1945   // After parsing command line options, update dimensions
1946   // First set any auto dimensions to terminal size, then apply full height logic
1947 
1948   // Clear auto flags if dimensions were explicitly parsed from options
1949   // This must happen before update_dimensions_to_terminal_size to prevent auto-detected
1950   // values from overwriting user-specified dimensions
1951   // Note: width=0 and height=0 are special values meaning "auto-detect", so don't clear auto flags for those
1952   if (opts.width != OPT_WIDTH_DEFAULT && opts.width != 0) {
1953     opts.auto_width = false;
1954   }
1955   if (opts.height != OPT_HEIGHT_DEFAULT && opts.height != 0) {
1956     opts.auto_height = false;
1957   }
1958   update_dimensions_to_terminal_size(&opts);
1959   update_dimensions_for_full_height(&opts);
1960   // Apply verbose level to log threshold
1961   // Each -V decreases the log level by 1 (showing more verbose output)
1962   // Minimum level is LOG_DEV (0)
1963   if (opts.verbose_level > 0) {
1964     log_level_t current_level = log_get_level();
1965     int new_level = (int)current_level - (int)opts.verbose_level;
1966     if (new_level < LOG_DEV) {
1967       new_level = LOG_DEV;
1968     }
1969     log_set_level((log_level_t)new_level);
1970   }
1971 
1972   // Check WEBCAM_DISABLED environment variable to enable test pattern mode
1973   // Useful for CI/CD and testing environments without a physical webcam
1974   const char *webcam_disabled = SAFE_GETENV("WEBCAM_DISABLED");
1975   if (webcam_disabled &&
1976       (strcmp(webcam_disabled, "1") == 0 || platform_strcasecmp(webcam_disabled, "true") == 0 ||
1977        platform_strcasecmp(webcam_disabled, "yes") == 0 || platform_strcasecmp(webcam_disabled, "on") == 0)) {
1978     opts.test_pattern = true;
1979   }
1980 
1981   // Apply --no-compress interaction with audio encoding
1982   if (opts.no_compress) {
1983     opts.encode_audio = false;
1984     log_debug("--no-compress set: disabling audio encoding");
1985   }
1986 
1987   // Set media_from_stdin flag if media_file is "-"
1988   if (opts.media_file[0] != '\0' && strcmp(opts.media_file, "-") == 0) {
1989     opts.media_from_stdin = true;
1990     log_debug("Media file set to stdin");
1991   }
1992 
1993   // Validate --seek option
1994   if (opts.media_seek_timestamp > 0.0) {
1995     // Can't seek stdin
1996     if (opts.media_from_stdin) {
1997       log_error("--seek cannot be used with stdin (--file -)");
1998       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
1999       return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2000     }
2001 
2002     // Require --file or --url
2003     if (opts.media_file[0] == '\0' && opts.media_url[0] == '\0') {
2004       log_error("--seek requires --file or --url");
2005       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
2006       return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2007     }
2008   }
2009 
2010   // Validate --pause option
2011   if (opts.pause) {
2012     // Require --file or --url (not webcam, not test pattern)
2013     if (opts.media_file[0] == '\0' && opts.media_url[0] == '\0') {
2014       log_error("--pause requires --file or --url");
2015       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
2016       return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2017     }
2018   }
2019 
2020   if (opts.media_url[0] != '\0') {
2021     // URL must be a valid HTTP(S) URL (YouTube URLs are HTTPS URLs)
2022     if (!url_is_valid(opts.media_url)) {
2023       log_error("--url must be a valid HTTP(S) URL: %s", opts.media_url);
2024       SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
2025       return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2026     }
2027 
2028     // Normalize bare URLs by prepending http:// if not present
2029     if (!strstr(opts.media_url, "://")) {
2030       char normalized_url[2048];
2031       int result = snprintf(normalized_url, sizeof(normalized_url), "http://%s", opts.media_url);
2032       if (result > 0 && result < (int)sizeof(normalized_url)) {
2033         SAFE_STRNCPY(opts.media_url, normalized_url, sizeof(opts.media_url));
2034       } else {
2035         log_error("Failed to normalize URL (too long): %s", opts.media_url);
2036         SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
2037         return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2038       }
2039     }
2040   }
2041 
2042   // ========================================================================
2043   // STAGE 8: Publish to RCU
2044   // ========================================================================
2045 
2046   // Adjust flip_x default: if --file or --url is provided, default flip_x to false
2047   // (file/URL content is typically not horizontally flipped, unlike webcam)
2048   if ((opts.media_file[0] != '\0' || opts.media_url[0] != '\0') && opts.flip_x) {
2049     opts.flip_x = false;
2050     log_debug("flip_x auto-adjusted to false for media file/URL");
2051   }
2052 
2053   // Save the quiet flag before publishing (RCU will be cleaned up before memory report runs)
2054 #if defined(DEBUG_MEMORY) && !defined(USE_MIMALLOC_DEBUG) && !defined(NDEBUG)
2055   bool quiet_for_memory_report = opts.quiet;
2056 #endif
2057 
2058   // Publish parsed options to RCU state (replaces options_state_populate_from_globals)
2059   // This makes the options visible to all threads via lock-free reads
2060   asciichat_error_t publish_result = options_state_set(&opts);
2061   if (publish_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2062     log_error("Failed to publish parsed options to RCU state: %d", publish_result);
2063     SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
2064     return publish_result;
2065   }
2066 
2067   // Now update debug memory quiet mode with the saved quiet value
2068 #if defined(DEBUG_MEMORY) && !defined(USE_MIMALLOC_DEBUG) && !defined(NDEBUG)
2069   debug_memory_set_quiet_mode(quiet_for_memory_report);
2070 #endif
2071 
2072   // ========================================================================
2073   // Apply color scheme to logging
2074   // ========================================================================
2075   // Now that options are parsed, set and apply the selected color scheme to logging
2076   if (opts.color_scheme_name[0] != '\0') {
2077     asciichat_error_t scheme_result = colorscheme_set_active_scheme(opts.color_scheme_name);
2078     if (scheme_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2079       const color_scheme_t *scheme = colorscheme_get_active_scheme();
2080       if (scheme) {
2081         log_set_color_scheme(scheme);
2082         log_debug("Color scheme applied: %s", opts.color_scheme_name);
2083       }
2084     } else {
2085       log_warn("Failed to apply color scheme: %s", opts.color_scheme_name);
2086     }
2087   }
2088 
2089   // ========================================================================
2090   // STAGE 8: Execute deferred actions (after all options parsed and published)
2091   // ========================================================================
2092   // Deferred actions (--list-webcams, --list-microphones, --list-speakers, --show-capabilities)
2093   // are executed here after all options are fully parsed and published via RCU.
2094   // This ensures action output reflects the final parsed state (e.g., final dimensions
2095   // for --show-capabilities).
2096   // Re-enable terminal output so deferred actions can print their results.
2097   // It was disabled earlier (STAGE 1C) to suppress log noise during option parsing.
2098   if (has_action) {
2099     log_set_terminal_output(true);
2100   }
2101   actions_execute_deferred();
2102   SAFE_FREE(allocated_mode_argv);
2103   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2104 }

asciichat_error_t options_set_int (const char *
  field_name, int value)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/options/options.h>


Check if an option was explicitly set via command-line.
    Convenience macro for checking if a specific option was explicitly provided
    by the user via command-line arguments (as opposed to using a default
    value).




Usage Examples:




// Check if user explicitly provided --width
if (IS_OPTION_EXPLICIT("width")) {


    printf("User set width explicitly\n");
}
// Use in conditional logic
if (!IS_OPTION_EXPLICIT("color") && terminal_is_dark_mode()) {


    // Color not set, apply dark mode defaults
}



Parameters


option_name The long name of the option (e.g.,
  "width", "port", "color")


Returns


true if option was explicitly set, false otherwise


Note


Option name must be a string literal matching the
  long_name from registry



Case-sensitive: must match exact option name




No locks needed - lock-free read via atomic pointer




Set a single option field (thread-safe, RCU-based)




Convenience function for updating a single field in the options
    struct. Uses RCU (copy-on-write) internally for thread-safe updates.




Example:




options_set_int("width", 120);
options_set_int("port", 8080);
options_set_bool("audio_enabled", true);



Thread Safety: Multiple writers are serialized with a
    mutex. Readers are never blocked (lock-free reads via GET_OPTION).

options_t options_t_new (void )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/options/options.h>


Initialize options by parsing command-line arguments.


Parameters


argc Argument count from main()


argv Argument vector from main()


is_client true if parsing client options, false for server options


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, ERROR_USAGE on parse
  errors


Parses command-line arguments and initializes all option global
    variables. This function must be called once at program startup before
    accessing any options.




Parsing Process:



  
1.

  
Parse command-line arguments using getopt() (POSIX-compliant)

  
2.

  
Validate option values (ranges, file existence, formats)

  
3.

  
Apply default values for unspecified options

  
4.

  
Perform mode-specific validation (client vs server)

  
5.

  
Initialize global option variables




Special Return Values:


    
  
  • ASCIICHAT_OK: Parsing succeeded (normal case)
    • 
  
  • ASCIICHAT_OK: Also returned for --help and
      --version (after printing info)
    • 
  
  • ERROR_USAGE: Parse error or invalid option (usage info should be
      printed)
    • 



Mode-Specific Behavior:


    
  
  • Client mode (is_client = true): Parses client-specific
      options (color mode, webcam, snapshot mode, etc.)
    • 
  
  • Server mode (is_client = false): Parses server-specific
      options (bind address, client keys whitelist, etc.)
    • 



Validation:


    
  
  • Numeric ranges (e.g., port 1-65535, webcam index >= 0)
    • 
  
  • File existence (key files, log files)
    • 
  
  • Format correctness (IP addresses, port numbers)
    • 
  
  • Mode compatibility (rejects client-only options in server mode)
    • 



Default Value Application: After parsing, unspecified
    options are set to defaults:


    
  
  • Terminal dimensions: Auto-detect or use
      OPT_WIDTH_DEFAULT/OPT_HEIGHT_DEFAULT
    • 
  
  • Network: localhost:27224
    • 
  
  • Webcam: Index 0 (first device)
    • 
  
  • Color mode: Auto-detect
    • 
  
  • Encryption: Enabled if keys found, disabled otherwise
    • 



Environment Variables: The following environment variables
    are checked during option parsing:



  
•

  
WEBCAM_DISABLED: When set to "1", "true",
      "yes", or "on", automatically enables test pattern
      mode (opt_test_pattern = true). Useful for CI/CD environments
      and testing without a physical webcam.




Example:








int main(int argc, char **argv) {


    // Parse options (client mode)


    asciichat_error_t err = options_init(argc, argv, true);


    if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {


        if (err == ERROR_USAGE) {


            usage(stderr, true);  // Print usage


        }


        return 1;


    }


    // Options are now available via global variables


    printf("Connecting to %s:%s\n", opt_address, opt_port);


    return 0;
}





Note


Must be called once at program startup before accessing
  options



Global option variables are initialized by this function




Returns ERROR_USAGE for invalid options (caller should
    print usage)




--help and --version cause early exit (function still returns
    ASCIICHAT_OK)




Create a new options_t struct with all defaults set




Initializes an options_t struct with all fields set to
    their default values from OPT_*_DEFAULT defines. This function is used
    internally by options_init() to ensure consistent default
    initialization before command-line parsing.




Behavior:


    
  
  • All numeric fields set to their OPT_*_DEFAULT values
    • 
  
  • All boolean fields set to their OPT_*_DEFAULT values
    • 
  
  • All string fields initialized with their default values
    • 
  
  • detected_mode set to MODE_INVALID (overwritten during parsing)
    • 



Usage: Typically called by options_init()
    internally. Can be used by custom code creating builder-based parsers for
    consistent defaults.




Returns


A new options_t struct with all defaults applied
  (stack-allocated)


Note


Returns a stack-allocated struct (caller should use
  immediately or copy)



All fields initialized to their OPT_*_DEFAULT constant values




Does not allocate memory (all fields are static arrays or
    primitives)




Definition at line 520 of file
    lib/options/options.c.

668   // server_key is already zeroed by memset
669   // client_keys is already zeroed by memset
670   // identity_keys array is already zeroed by memset
671   opts.num_identity_keys = 0;
672   opts.require_server_verify = OPT_REQUIRE_SERVER_VERIFY_DEFAULT;
673   opts.require_client_verify = OPT_REQUIRE_CLIENT_VERIFY_DEFAULT;
674 
675   // ============================================================================
676   // CONFIGURATION CATEGORY - Application configuration options
677   // ============================================================================
678   // config_file is already zeroed by memset
679 
680   // ============================================================================
681   // DATABASE CATEGORY - Database and persistent storage options
682   // ============================================================================
683   // discovery_database_path is already zeroed by memset (set above in NETWORK)
684 
685   // ============================================================================
686   // Internal/System Fields
687   // ============================================================================
688   // session_string is already zeroed by memset
689   // detected_mode is already zeroed by memset
690 
691   return opts;
692 }

  free_parsed_sections)

684   if (!filepath || !num_sections) {
685     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
686     return NULL;
687   }
688 
689   FILE *f = platform_fopen("file_stream", filepath, "r");
690   if (!f) {
691     SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to open file: %s", filepath);
692     return NULL;
693   }
694 
695   parsed_section_t *sections = NULL;
696   asciichat_error_t err = manpage_parser_parse_file(f, &sections, num_sections);
697   fclose(f);
698 
699   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
700     return NULL;
701   }
702 
703   return sections;
704 }
